text: Stood up a new systematic-review study in the working workspace on whether the fascial/connective-tissue system (tension, sensory innervation, myofascial state) is associated with emotional and psychological outcomes in humans. After orienting in the existing engine (schemas, screen.py, validate.py, build_views.py, build_report.py, and a prior mesothelioma review), the human's PICO question was refined and scope locked to interventions-only, any human, fascia-explicit, under the slug fascia-emotion. Wrote and schema-validated protocol.json, then ran four complementary PubMed queries via pubmed_search.py (broad, physiological markers, named modalities, anxiety/stress) for recall â€” the broad query returned 350 records. Work ended mid-search-tuning: the named-modalities query was a firehose (16,967 hits, truncated at 500), so it was being re-run tightened to specific modality names (Rolfing, structural integration, Bowen technique) to get a complete non-truncated set. No screening, extraction, views, or report was reached.
